title: ranunculus rigidulus
intro: Ranunculus rigidulus, also known as the stiff-flower or rigid buttercup, is a perennial plant species within the Ranunculus genus. It is native to North America and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The plant's leaves and flowers have been used to treat a variety of health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its health benefits in reducing inflammation and promoting healing. Ranunculus rigidulus can be prepared for herbal use through infusions, tinctures, and salves, making it a versatile and valuable plant in natural medicine.
